strengths: Over 15 years of hands-on engineering experience, including building low-latency microservices handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby.
Relevant exposure to financial software and investment practices through previous engineering roles at RIDE Capital and Optilyz.
Recent, hands-on development experience with AI-agent infrastructure and Model Context Protocol (MCP) tooling at re:cinq.
concerns: Completely lacks the required executive leadership experience, having never served as a CTO, managed managers, or scaled an engineering team from 10 to 30+ people.
Fails the hard requirement for production experience building and operating multi-agent AI systems, with AI exposure limited to a very recent individual contributor role.
Missing required experience in governing outsourced delivery partners, establishing compliance infrastructure, and managing board-level technical communications.
response.justification: Aisha Okonkwo is a highly experienced software craftsman with strong individual contributor skills in backend performance and microservices, but she falls fundamentally short of the executive requirements for a CTO. She lacks the mandated 5+ years of senior technical leadership, having no demonstrated experience scaling engineering organizations, communicating with a board, or managing outsourced teams. Furthermore, while she has recently touched AI-agent tooling as a Staff Engineer, she lacks the required production track record of architecting large-scale, multi-agent AI frameworks for high-stakes fintech environments, making her an exceptionally weak fit for this executive leadership role.
